title: 2023 AMC 12A Problems/Problem 23
problem: How many ordered pairs of positive real numbers $(a,b)$ satisfy the equation \[(1+2a)(2+2b)(2a+b) = 32ab?\] $\textbf{(A) }0\qquad\textbf{(B) }1\qquad\textbf{(C) }2\qquad\textbf{(D) }3\qquad\textbf{(E) }\text{an infinite number}$
solution: Using the AM-GM inequality on the two terms in each factor on the left-hand side, we get \[(1+2a)(2+2b)(2a+b) \ge 8\sqrt{2a \cdot 4b \cdot 2ab}= 32ab,\] This means the equality condition must be satisfied. Therefore, we must have $1 = 2a = b$ , so the only solution is $\boxed{\textbf{(B) }1}$ . ~ semistevehan
